summary: Audit superadmin control/visibility over logs, DB, async tasks, queries, server; report gaps + perf recommendations

result.summary: The superadmin observability surface is strong on infrastructure snapshots (system/worker/DB/slow-query/jobs) and has a genuinely good cross-tenant admin-action audit log, but it is blind to the two things operators most need during an incident: application errors and history/trends. Every 500, unhandled rejection, and crash is console-only (a fully-built ErrorTrackingService sits unwired as dead code, with no persistence and no UI). Almost every live metric is an instantaneous, per-PM2-worker snapshot with no retention, no cross-worker aggregation, and no alerting, so gradual degradation (heap/RSS creep, disk/uploads growth, pool saturation, job stalls) is invisible until users complain. Rich auth data (failed logins, IP/UA) already exists but has no superadmin cross-tenant view, no rate-limit-hit logging, and no account lockout. The highest-leverage fixes are cross-cutting: persist+wire error tracking, add a request-context/request-id layer, add a metrics-history writer, and add one threshold-based alerting path — each pays off across multiple domains.

coverageByDomain.oneLine: Single choke point exists but only console.errors; nothing persisted, a complete ErrorTrackingService is unwired dead code, and there is no superadmin error page — errors are effectively invisible.

securityAuditLog already captures logins/failed-logins with IP/UA and rate limiting exists, but there is no superadmin cross-tenant view, 429 hits are unlogged, and there is no account lockout or HTTP access log.
Cross-tenant superAdminAuditLog model + read API + filterable UI page exist; gaps are missing coverage on sensitive mutations (Stripe/Twilio), weak filters/export, and no retention.
Pool snapshot, total DB size, slow digests and health are live, but no per-table breakdown, no live PROCESSLIST/kill, no connection-saturation history, and no backup/replication status.

runJob wraps 11 schedulers with a UI table and leader election, but the panel is per-worker (shows empty on non-leader), 3 jobs are unwrapped, and there is no queue, manual trigger, overdue detection, or alerting.
Slow-query ring buffer + perf_schema digests + a good UI page exist, but no route/tenant attribution, no persistence/trend, no EXPLAIN, per-worker fragmentation, and no N+1 detection.
Solid instantaneous system+per-worker snapshots via Redis, but no historical time-series, no alerting/thresholds, no event-loop lag, and no crash/restart visibility.

topRecommendations.title: Persist and wire application error tracking + add a superadmin Errors page
topRecommendations.why: The single biggest blind spot: every 500, unhandled rejection and uncaught exception is console-only across 2-3 rotating per-worker PM2 logs, so an operator cannot see a single backend exception, error rate, or affected tenant without SSHing to prod. A fully-built ErrorTrackingService (fingerprinting, per-tenant/endpoint stats, spike/critical alerts) already exists but is never called.
topRecommendations.what: Add an error_events table and persist from the ONE choke point (ApiResponseHandler.error 500 branch + the two server.ts crash handlers), reusing the orphaned ErrorTrackingService's fingerprint/pattern logic as a write-through store. Wire trackError() with tenantId/userId/route/method/ip/UA, expose GET /observability/errors (+resolve) on the live api/superadmin router, and add ErrorsPage.tsx (error-rate sparkline, top patterns with resolve, filterable recent table with stack drill-down).

topRecommendations.title: Add an AsyncLocalStorage request context + request-id (attribution/correlation layer)
topRecommendations.why: One small piece of infra unblocks three high-severity gaps across domains: slow queries currently carry no route/tenant (can't locate the code to fix), N+1 over-fetch on ~144 list endpoints is undetectable, and reported errors have no traceable id. This is the highest-leverage multiplier in the list.
topRecommendations.what: Add early middleware that seeds AsyncLocalStorage with {requestId (x-request-id), path, method, tenantId, userId} and echoes requestId in the 500 body. Have the Sequelize benchmark logger read it so each captured slow query carries its endpoint (add a 'Ruta' column), maintain a per-request query counter that records a 'high query count' event on request end, and store requestId on the error_event row.

topRecommendations.title: Add a metrics-history writer (leader-elected 60s snapshot) with sparkline charts
topRecommendations.why: Nearly every live metric — system, workers, DB pool, slow-query totals, job counters — is instantaneous with a 30s Redis TTL, so memory/RSS creep, disk/uploads growth, pool saturation, and load trends are only visible if someone is staring at the page. No way to correlate a slowdown with when it started or a deploy. This is the biggest gap for operating toward 100k users and simultaneously solves cross-worker aggregation.
topRecommendations.what: A runJob('MetricsSnapshot') every 60s (reusing the existing heartbeat-lock so one box records) appends a system+worker+pool+slow rollup to a metricsSnapshot table (or Redis sorted-set ring) with a ~14-day prune. Add GET /observability/system/history?metric=&hours= feeding sparklines on ObservabilityPage, and fold slow-query and job-counter history into the same store.

topRecommendations.title: Add a unified threshold evaluator + alerting over the existing broadcast/FCM path
topRecommendations.why: Nothing anywhere evaluates a bound and notifies — degradation and outages are discovered by tenant complaints, not the platform. This covers disk-full on /uploads (a P0), RAM/heap exhaustion, DB pool waiting>0, job lastStatus==='error', and 500 spikes in one mechanism, closing the 'alerting' gap flagged as high/medium in four separate domains.
topRecommendations.what: In the same 60s snapshot job, evaluate configurable thresholds (disk usedPct, host mem, process heapUsedPct, load/cores, DB pool waiting>0, any job in error/overdue, error-rate spike, critical error) and fan out one superadmin notification via the existing broadcastPush/FCM + notification center. Surface an alerts panel + editable thresholds table.

topRecommendations.title: Aggregate jobs and slow queries across PM2 workers via Redis (+ wrap the 3 unwrapped jobs)
topRecommendations.why: The Jobs panel is per-worker and schedulers run only on the leader, so a request routed to a non-leader worker shows an empty/misleading Jobs table — the whole background-work surface is untrustworthy. Slow-query capture is likewise fragmented per worker. Three schedulers (RepeatedLateness, ExpiredInvitesCleanup, PlatformEventsCleanup) are not wrapped at all and are completely invisible.
topRecommendations.what: Publish JobStats to Redis (obs:job:<name>, mirroring workerMetrics) from runJob() and have observabilityService.jobs() merge the Redis hash; do the same for captured slow queries. Wrap the three missing schedulers in runJob() so they get last-run/error tracking, and add an in-flight re-entrancy guard + overdue/next-run metadata.

topRecommendations.title: Add a cross-tenant auth/security events page + log rate-limit (429) hits
topRecommendations.why: Fleet-wide auth data (logins, login_failed with IP/UA, device events) already exists but is only reachable through a per-tenant CRM endpoint, so the one operator who can see across tenants is blind to credential-stuffing/brute-force campaigns. The primary brute-force signal — repeated 429 throttling — is dropped entirely because the rate limiter sets no handler. High security value for low effort.
topRecommendations.what: Add GET /observability/auth-events querying securityAuditLog cross-tenant (event/outcome/ip/email/tenant/time filters + a 'top failed-login IPs/emails 24h' rollup) and an 'Accesos y Auth' page reusing the findAndCountAll shape. Add a handler to createRateLimiter that logs {event:'rate_limited',outcome:'failure',ip,route} before returning 429, and surface a throttled-IPs panel.

topRecommendations.title: Add DB per-table breakdown + live PROCESSLIST (with guarded kill)
topRecommendations.why: dbSize is a single grand total and stats() counts only 8 hardcoded models, so superadmin cannot see which table is bloating or growing. During an incident (a query pinning CPU, a lock storm, connections exhausted) there is no in-panel way to identify or stop the offender — must SSH into MySQL.
topRecommendations.what: Add GET /observability/db/tables (information_schema.tables: rows, data_length, index_length, total, data_free, sorted by size) and GET /observability/db/processlist (information_schema.PROCESSLIST WHERE command<>'Sleep', flag time>N long-runners), plus an optional guarded POST /observability/db/kill/:id (KILL QUERY behind extra confirm + audit entry). Also add SHOW GLOBAL STATUS Threads_connected/max_connections headroom to dbPerformance().

topRecommendations.title: Harden audit coverage: auto-audit middleware + fill sensitive-mutation gaps + better filters/export
topRecommendations.why: The most security-relevant changes are unaudited: PUT /settings/stripe (rotates Stripe SECRET keys) and 2 of 6 Twilio mutations have zero writeAudit calls, defeating the audit log for exactly the high-risk actions it exists to catch. Coverage depends on each route remembering to call writeAudit. The query/UI also can't filter by time or free text and hides whole action categories behind a hardcoded dropdown.
topRecommendations.what: Add an audit middleware on the /api/superadmin router that logs every non-GET by default (action derived from method+path) so new routes are audited by construction; add explicit redacted writeAudit to settings/twilio/reset-slow-queries. Add from/to/targetType/statusCode/keyword filters + a SELECT DISTINCT action list to auditLog() and the page, plus a streamed CSV export and a retention prune job.

topRecommendations.title: Add account lockout + superadmin lock/unlock and force-logout controls
topRecommendations.why: Protection is purely IP-based (100 sign-ins/15min); there is no per-account failed-attempt counter, so distributed credential-stuffing against a high-value account (tenant owner/superadmin), or attacks from an allowlisted IP, are unbounded. During a compromise there is also no way to see or terminate a user's active sessions except a raw DB update to jwtTokenInvalidBefore.
topRecommendations.what: Add users.failedLoginCount + lockedUntil, increment on login_failed and enforce a temporary lock in authService.signin, and expose a superadmin 'currently locked accounts' list + lock/unlock control. Add GET /observability/sessions (login events joined with guardDevice) and a superadmin action to bump jwtTokenInvalidBefore to force-logout a chosen user/tenant.

topRecommendations.title: Add EXPLAIN endpoint + missing-index/full-scan signals to query perf
topRecommendations.why: Operators can see that a query is slow but not why — there is no EXPLAIN, and the digest view omits perf_schema scan columns, so index gaps stay hidden and tuning is guesswork. This is the difference between observing a problem and being able to fix it.
topRecommendations.what: Add POST /observability/explain {sql} (SELECT-only guard, EXPLAIN FORMAT=JSON) with an 'Explicar' row action. Extend the digest SELECT with sum_no_index_used/sum_no_good_index_used/sum_rows_sent, compute an examined:sent ratio and render 'sin índice'/'scan' badges, and add GET /observability/db/indexes from sys.schema_unused_indexes + sys.schema_redundant_indexes.

topRecommendations.title: Make uncaughtException exit the process (+ persist crash) and add event-loop lag/restart visibility
topRecommendations.why: After an uncaught exception the worker keeps serving requests in a corrupted state (leaked connections, half-open transactions) instead of letting PM2 restart cleanly. Separately, process.uptime() makes a crash-looping worker indistinguishable from a healthy one, and host loadavg hides Node event-loop saturation. These are small, high-value hardening wins.
topRecommendations.what: After persisting the error, call process.exit(1) in the server.ts uncaughtException handler (flag as backend-only, not a superadmin/ change). Add perf_hooks.monitorEventLoopDelay() to buildSnapshot() in workerMetrics so each worker card shows event-loop lag, and surface pm2 jlist restart_time/unstable_restarts to flag recently-restarted/crash-looping workers.

topRecommendations.title: Introduce a real job queue (BullMQ on existing Redis) with a queues observability page
topRecommendations.why: Heavy async work runs inline/synchronously on the single leader worker: push fan-out processes 500-token batches inside the request, and CustomerSummaryDigest loops all tenants on one tick, with no retry/backoff/dead-letter. A slow FCM call stalls the request or the whole scheduler tick, failed pushes are silently dropped, and queue depth/failures are invisible. Highest-value reliability fix but larger effort, so ranked below the quick wins.
topRecommendations.what: Move push fan-out, digests and email onto BullMQ (Redis already present) with retries/backoff/dead-letter and cross-worker concurrency, and add GET /observability/queues (waiting/active/failed/delayed depth + retry/drain controls) plus a job-failure watchdog that alerts via the rank-4 path.
